report_name: GitHub Confirms Source Code Breach Via Compromised Employee Device and Malicious VS Code Extension

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The attack targeted a developer's Visual Studio Code extension, a key component of the software supply chain, to gain initial access to an employee's device.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: A malicious Visual Studio Code extension likely stole credentials from the compromised local system to gain access to internal repositories.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The primary goal of the malicious extension was likely to steal application access tokens that grant access to GitHub's internal source code repositories.

attack_patterns.name: Data Staged
attack_patterns.description: Attackers identified and staged approximately 3,800 internal code repositories for exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: The exfiltration of 3,800 repositories was likely automated using scripts that leveraged stolen access tokens to clone repositories in bulk.

malware.name: Malicious Visual Studio Code Extension
malware.description: A weaponized Visual Studio Code extension used to compromise a GitHub employee's device. The extension was used to steal credentials or access tokens and exfiltrate internal source code repositories.

tools.name: Visual Studio Code
tools.description: A legitimate code editor that was targeted by the attackers. A malicious extension for VS Code was used as the entry vector for the compromise.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: The victim organization, a code hosting platform, whose internal source code was stolen via a compromised employee device.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Parent company of the victim organization, GitHub.

indicators.name: Unapproved Visual Studio Code Extension Installation
indicators.pattern:
title: Unapproved Visual Studio Code Extension Installation
id: 5f9b1c3a-8d7e-4f6a-9b1c-3d8e7f6a9b1c
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the installation of a Visual Studio Code extension via the command line. 
  This can be used to spot the installation of potentially malicious or unapproved extensions on developer workstations, as seen in the GitHub breach.
references:
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/25th-may-threat-intelligence-report/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-26
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\Code.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '--install-extension'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the installation of Visual Studio Code extensions, which could indicate the introduction of a malicious or unapproved extension as described in the attack vector.

indicators.name: Mass Git Clone or Fetch Operation
indicators.pattern:
title: Mass Git Clone or Fetch Operation By Single User
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects an unusually high number of git clone or fetch operations from a single user account over a short period. 
  This is indicative of bulk data exfiltration from a source code repository, similar to the theft of 3,800 repositories from GitHub.
  This rule is conceptual and requires a log source that can aggregate version control system audit logs (e.g., GitHub Enterprise Audit Log, GitLab logs).
references:
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/25th-may-threat-intelligence-report/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-26
tags:
  - attack.t1074
  - attack.t1119
level: high
logsource:
  category: application
  product: github
detection:
  selection:
    event_type: 'git.clone'
  condition: selection | count(repository) by user > 100 over 1h
indicators.description: Detects mass Git clone or fetch operations from a single account, which could indicate bulk exfiltration of source code as seen in the breach.

relationships.source_name: Unapproved Visual Studio Code Extension Installation
Mass Git Clone or Fetch Operation
